commit.message:
feat: add Instagram-style activity share cards with multiple variants (#187)

Adds shareable activity cards for Instagram stories with three style options:
- Activity card (points & metrics)
- Leaderboard card (rank position + total points)
- Streak card (current streak with fire visual)

Uses Canvas API to generate 1080x1920 images with the brand's dark/bold aesthetic.
Share dialog with style picker, download as PNG, and Web Share API integration.
Integrated into both the activity feed and activity detail page.

commit.message:
Add extensive unit tests for project-store and pty-manager (#660) (#715)

## Summary
- Adds comprehensive edge case and error handling tests for
`project-store.ts` and `pty-manager.ts`
- Addresses GitHub issue #660 (TC-26): Main process services lacked
tests covering filesystem errors, race conditions, and PTY lifecycle
scenarios
- Increases test count from 79 → 135 across both files (+56 new tests)

## Changes

### project-store.test.ts (+30 new tests)
- **Filesystem error handling**: ENOSPC on write, EACCES on read/write,
ENOENT on readdir, EPERM on unlink, EXDEV on rename
- **Corrupt sidecar recovery**: Graceful handling of malformed preserved
settings JSON
- **setIcon**: File copy, extension handling (`.webp`, no extension →
`.png`), old icon cleanup
- **saveCroppedIcon**: Data URL prefix stripping, buffer writing, store
field update
- **update edge cases**: `displayName` set/clear, `orchestrator` set,
`icon` set/clear with file cleanup, multi-field updates
- **remove edge cases**: Nonexistent ID, empty project list
- **reorder edge cases**: Empty orderedIds, duplicate IDs, unknown IDs
- **readIconData**: All MIME types (`.ico`, `.gif`, `.webp`, unknown ext
defaults to `image/png`)

### pty-manager.test.ts (+26 new tests)
- **PTY spawn failure recovery**: `ENOMEM`, `ENOENT` errors propagate
correctly; session not registered on failure
- **Re-spawn failure cleanup**: Existing session killed before spawn
failure propagates
- **onData/onExit identity guards**: Stale callbacks from replaced
sessions are correctly ignored (uses distinct mock process objects)
- **onExit lifecycle**: Callback invocation with buffer content,
broadcast with exit code and last output, session cleanup
- **spawnShell lifecycle**: Data buffering via `appendToBuffer`, IPC
broadcast, exit cleanup and broadcast
- **Buffer compaction**: Verifies array compaction after
COMPACT_THRESHOLD evictions
- **killAll edge cases**: Dead processes (write throws), multiple
session cleanup
- **gracefulKill with dead process**: Initial write failure caught,
escalation timer errors handled gracefully

## Test Plan
- [x] All 135 tests pass across both files (61 project-store + 74
pty-manager)
- [x] No new lint errors introduced
- [x] Pre-existing failures in renderer store tests and typecheck are
unrelated

## Manual Validation
No manual validation needed — this is a test-only change with no
production code modifications.

Closes #660

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
v16 GA plan: horizon-aware trot with trajectory shape + CoM shift genes

19-gene genome: gait timing (4), trajectory shape (3, needs L4 cascade),
body pose/CoM (3, uses existing GaitParams fields), navigation (3),
horizon model weights (6). Multiplicative fitness: speed × stability ×
grip × turn_ability. Fall = zero.

Cascade initiated for parameterized swing trajectory (L4 Bezier control
points currently hardcoded). Body lean genes use existing GaitParams
fields (body_roll, body_pitch, body_y_offset) — no cascade needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix naive/aware datetime mismatch in episode tracker

Nest event timestamps are timezone-aware but datetime.now() is naive,
causing TypeError when calculating episode duration. Strip tzinfo from
the event timestamp on init.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Replace 598px/297px fixed columns with 2fr/1fr proportional units
- Same fix for rich-block (1fr 1fr) and text-img (1fr 1fr) grids
- Remove redundant max-width: 598px from rich-text
- Remove duplicate margin-top on text-cell paragraphs (flex gap handles spacing)
- Bump components.css version to v55 in tangram.html

fix: replace .toBeTruthy() with specific assertions across test files (#714)

## Summary
- Replace 58 weak `.toBeTruthy()` assertions across 22 test files with
more specific matchers
- Fixes #657 — improves test effectiveness by catching wrong types and
unexpected truthy values

## Changes
Replaced `.toBeTruthy()` with contextually appropriate assertions:

| Replacement | Count | Used For |
|---|---|---|
| `.toBeInTheDocument()` | 30 | DOM element queries (`screen.getByText`,
`querySelector`, etc.) |
| `.toBeDefined()` | 27 | Variable existence checks (`findItem()`
results, mock instances, callback refs) |
| `.toBeGreaterThan(0)` | 1 | Bitwise `compareDocumentPosition` result |

### Files Modified (22)
- `themes.test.ts` — theme property existence
- `CommandPalette.test.tsx` — DOM element presence
- `use-command-source.test.ts` — command source item lookups
- `fuzzy-match.test.ts` — filter result existence
- `PopoutWindow.test.tsx` — functional setState call existence
- `PopoutHubView.test.tsx` — DOM container queries
- `PopoutHubPane.test.tsx` — drag overlay DOM check
- `McpJsonSection.test.tsx` — error message DOM presence
- `ProjectRail.test.tsx` — container DOM queries
- `ConfigChangesDialog.test.tsx` — spinner and backdrop DOM
- `PluginDialog.test.tsx` — dialog DOM presence
- `AgentTerminal.test.tsx` — xterm mock instance existence
- `toastStore.test.ts` — toast ID existence
- `PluginPermissions.test.tsx` — popup style value
- `ProjectSettings.test.tsx` — SVG element presence
- `OrchestratorSettingsView.test.tsx` — document position comparison
- `AboutSettingsView.test.tsx` — async DOM text presence
- `AgentList.test.tsx` — dropdown button existence
- `ShellTerminal.test.tsx` — terminal mock instances
- `hub-sync.test.ts` — pane tree leaf existence
- `SleepingAgent.test.tsx` — color dot DOM presence
- `plugin-api-factory.test.ts` — dialog DOM presence

## Test Plan
- [x] All 5710 tests pass (3 pre-existing failures unrelated to this
change)
- [x] TypeScript typecheck passes (1 pre-existing error in
`file-watch-service.ts`)
- [x] Lint passes (2 pre-existing errors in `PluginListSettings.tsx`)
- [x] Zero remaining `.toBeTruthy()` calls in test files
- [x] Each replacement is contextually correct — DOM checks use
`toBeInTheDocument()`, variable checks use `toBeDefined()`, numeric
checks use `toBeGreaterThan()`

## Manual Validation
No manual validation needed — this is a test-only change with no runtime
impact.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Mac IME 입력 시 NFD 형식으로 들어오는 한글이 NFC 데이터와 매칭 실패하는 버그 수정
검색 쿼리와 비교 대상 모두 normalize("NFC") 처리

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add LCCN and OCLC identifier support for older book imports

- Detect LCCN (letter+digit and post-2001 10-digit) and OCLC (ocm/ocn/on prefix)
  in lib/isbn.ts and in both client pages
- Extend /api/isbn to route LCCN through Open Library (LCCN: bibkey),
  Google Books (lccn: query), and LOC item API
- Extend /api/isbn to route OCLC through Open Library (OCLC: bibkey)
  with Google Books fallback using extracted ISBN or title
- openLibraryLookupByBibkey extracts ISBN-10/13 from OL response for
  downstream cover and Google Books enrichment
- Client smartAddOrSearch and smartLookup now route LCCN/OCLC to the
  identifier lookup path rather than falling through to title search

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Starter ($0): 500 min, 5 campaigns, 10 numbers, 2 users

commit.message:
test: fix flaky timing tests and reduce excessive loop counts (#520)

Flakiness fixes: add timing buffers to timestamp boundary checks in
achievement and deep discovery tests to prevent failures under load.

Performance fixes: reduce loop counts in deterministic/probabilistic
tests (2000→100/200, 5000→1000) where fewer iterations suffice.

Verified with 10 consecutive full test runs, 0 failures.

fix(ui): replace naked IDs with CopyButton and use Hash icon (#741)

## What
Replace raw entity ID text rendering with `CopyButton` across the UI,
and change the `CopyButton` icon from generic `Copy` to `Hash` (#) to
visually distinguish "copy ID" from other copy actions.

## Why
App cards rendered full agent IDs inline (e.g.,
`agent_01933b5a000070008000000000000101`) causing layout overflow. Other
pages (durable dashboard, workers, session tooltips, message info) also
displayed raw or truncated IDs without a copy mechanism.

## How
- **`CopyButton` icon**: `Copy` → `Hash` — the `#` symbol is a developer
convention for identifiers
- **App card** (`apps/page.tsx`): removed inline `agent_id` text, added
`CopyButton` for app ID next to title, kept generic `Copy` icon for
webhook URL
- **Session tooltip** (`session-card.tsx`): replaced raw `session.id`
with `CopyButton`
- **Message info tooltip** (`message-info-icon.tsx`): replaced raw
`event.id` with `CopyButton`
- **Durable dashboard** (`durable/page.tsx`): replaced sliced
worker/workflow IDs with `CopyButton`
- **Workers table** (`durable/workers/page.tsx`): replaced sliced worker
ID with `CopyButton`
- **Spec** (`specs/code-organization.md`): documented Hash icon
convention

## Risk
Low — UI-only changes, no backend/API modifications. All changes swap
text for an existing, well-tested component.

## Checklist
- [x] Lint passes (0 errors)
- [x] UI builds successfully
- [x] No new dependencies
- [x] Spec updated
- [x] Follows existing CopyButton pattern from agent/harness cards

---------

commit.message:
Scale API to 35 workers for 80-core server (~10x throughput)

- Workers: 3 → 35 (35 × 2 ONNX threads = 70 cores, 10 left for OS/nginx/pg)
- OMP_NUM_THREADS: 4 → 2 per worker
- DB pool: 5 per worker + 3 overflow = 280 total (pg max 500)
- API memory limit: 32G → 48G (35 workers × ~700MB each)
- PostgreSQL max_connections: 300 → 500
- Health check timeout: 180s → 300s (35 workers loading models)

feat: email notifications + client self-service portal

Email system (Resend REST API — no package required):
- Client confirmation email with reservation details + portal link
- Admin notification on new booking, modification, cancellation
- Client confirmation emails for modifications and cancellations

Server-side reservation store:
- lib/server-reservations.ts: JSON file-based store (/data/reservations.json)
- Synced between client portal actions and admin dashboard

API routes:
- POST /api/reservations/notify — save to server + send both emails
- GET  /api/reservations/list   — admin reads server-side reservations
- GET  /api/reservations/[token] — client views reservation (HMAC verified)
- PATCH /api/reservations/[token] — client modifies (sends emails to both)
- DELETE /api/reservations/[token] — client cancels (sends emails to both)

Client portal /reservation/[token]:
- Secure HMAC-signed token in email link
- View reservation details with status badge
- Modify: dates, times, pickup/dropoff locations
- Cancel: with optional reason field
- Success screens with email confirmation notice

Booking flow:
- saveReservation (localStorage) + notify API called together
- Email sending is non-blocking (fire-and-forget)

commit.message:
Implement tup.config / @() config variable support

Adds support for tup.config configuration variables:

1. Config file parsing: Reads CONFIG_VAR=value and "# CONFIG_VAR is
   not set" (=n) entries from tup.config at project root.

2. @(VAR) expansion: Config variables are expanded in Tupfile content
   alongside regular $(VAR) variables.

3. vardict: Writes .tup/vardict with parsed config for the test
   framework's vardict_exist/vardict_no_exist assertions.

Note: Incremental rebuild on config change (re-parse when tup.config
is modified) requires change detection, not yet implemented.
